name: Safe DLL Search Mode Disabled
description: Safe DLL search mode is disabled, which could allow an attacker to perform a DLL Side-Loading attack. This prompt helps investigate the event by checking the registry key `SafeDllSearchMode` and its value. If the value is `0`, it means that the safe DLL search mode is disabled. The DLL Side-Loading attack can be used to achieve persistence or defense evasion. This prompt can be used to investigate this type of activity.

prompts.prompt: Investigate the security event with Event ID {event_id} from the WindowsSysmon logs. The `SafeDllSearchMode` registry key has been modified to a value of `0x00000000`, indicating that safe DLL search mode has been disabled. The event occurred at `{timestamp}` on the host `{hostname}`. The process that made the change was `{process_name}` with Process ID `{process_id}`. Analyze the command line (`{command_line}`) for any suspicious arguments and review the user `{user}` associated with the activity. Provide a summary of your findings and determine if this is a legitimate configuration change or a potential malicious activity. If malicious, identify the potential threat and suggest immediate mitigation steps.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Sysmon Event ID for the registry value modification (should be 13).

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname where the event was detected.

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process that modified the registry key.

parameters.name: process_id
parameters.description: The Process ID of the process that modified the registry key.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The command line of the process that modified the registry key.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process was executed.
